body.title: [sprint] Fix stale/buggy lines in DS.0 builder system prompt (coding.md)
body.intention: I intend that DS.0's builder system prompt (coding.md) matches current reality — filesystem memory (not the retired psql palace), targeted tests (not the red full suite), and the correct commit/push/close ownership — so the prompt doesn't steer a build into dead commands and false failures.
body.description:
Verified bugs in unseen_university/devices/inference/prompts/coding.md (the builder system prompt that ran today): (1) L20 `memory_get -> psql ... memory_palace` — the palace is RETIRED; memory is the filesystem store (devlab/runtime/memory/, grep-able) — the model would run a dead query. (2) L63 step-4 runs the WHOLE suite `pytest tests/` which is RED right now (granny_bus_dispatch fails + igor collection errors), so the 'green run required' gate trips on failures DS didn't cause -> false escalate; use TARGETED tests for the changed files. (3) L64 `git push origin main` has NO askpass guard (DISPLAY= SSH_ASKPASS_REQUIRE=never GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 GIT_ASKPASS=/bin/true) -> hangs on a GUI/2FA dialog (feedback_push_every_ticket). (4) Steps 5-6 (model git-push + cc_queue close) are VESTIGIAL/DOUBLE — the listener _post_result owns the close (see T-ds-listener-proof-on-close); reconcile who commits/pushes/closes (model vs listener) so it's not done twice. Confirm each against the observation run (T-ds-fullcycle-observe-run).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/prompts/coding.md.
**Design rules:** filesystem-memory-canonical; targeted-tests-not-full-suite; push-askpass-guard.
**Scope boundary:** IN — fix L20 (filesystem grep), L63 (targeted tests), L64 (askpass guard IF the model pushes), reconcile steps 5-6 with the listener. OUT — the prompt REDESIGN / scaffolding change (T-ds-builder-prompt-redesign-v2); the listener close fix (T-ds-listener-proof-on-close).
**Test plan:** no automated tests (prompt is markdown). Acceptance: grep coding.md shows no psql/memory_palace, no bare `pytest tests/`, a guarded push (or push removed if the listener pushes); an observation full-cycle run no longer dies on those steps.
**Completion criteria:** coding.md has no dead psql memory_get, uses targeted tests, guards the push, and commit/push/close ownership is unambiguous vs the listener.
**Rollback:** git revert.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
